Meaning: Represents a 32‑bit signed integer. Used for whole numbers.
Represents a 64‑bit double‑precision floating‑point number. Used for decimals with high precision.
Represents a 32‑bit single‑precision floating‑point number. Used for decimals with less precision.
Represents a 128‑bit precise decimal value. Best for financial calculations.
Represents a Boolean value: true or false.
Base type for all data types in C#. Can store any type of data.
Implicitly typed variable. Type is determined at compile time.
Type is resolved at runtime. Useful for flexible scenarios.
Represents a 64‑bit signed integer. Used for very large whole numbers.
Represents a 16‑bit signed integer. Smaller range than int.
